howItWorks.intro: This merch profit margin calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Profit Per Unit = Sale Price - Unit Cost - Shipping - Platform Fees
Profit Margin = (Profit Per Unit / Sale Price) x 100
Platform Fees = Sale Price x Fee %
Total Profit = Profit Per Unit x Quantity
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is a good profit margin for band merchandise?
faq.answer: Most successful merch operations target 40-60% profit margins. T-shirts typically achieve 50-70% margins (cost $5-$10, sell $25-$35), while premium items like hoodies have lower margins but higher dollar profits.

faq.question: Should I include shipping in the sale price?
faq.answer: For online sales, offering free shipping (built into the price) often increases conversion rates. At live shows, shipping is not a factor. Platform fees from Shopify, Bandcamp, or PayPal should always be factored into your pricing.

faq.question: How many merch items should I order?
faq.answer: Start with smaller runs (50-100 units) to test demand. Focus on your best-selling sizes (M, L, XL). At live shows, plan for roughly 10-15% of attendees to buy merch. Overordering ties up cash in unsold inventory.
